experiments.method: Online survey distributed to game theory professors via academic networks (EA Forum, Reddit r/gametheory, academic mailing lists, Twitter academic community)
experiments.failure_threshold: <20% express need OR >=60% say current tools are sufficient
experiments.decision_rule: If success threshold met → advance H-EDU-1 to validation; if failure threshold met → weaken H-EDU-1 and recycle to Phase 3; if inconclusive → redesign survey
experiments.risk_check: No external outreach authorized without user approval. No PII collected. No production changes.
experiments.instrumentation: Manual recording in experiments.json. Survey results stored in spreadsheet. No automated tracking.
experiments.claim_being_tested: >=40% of game theory educators say they need an integrated platform combining game theory analysis, evidence retrieval, and forecast governance

experiments.method: Create education-focused landing page. Drive traffic via academic Twitter, Reddit, LinkedIn. Measure email signups.
experiments.decision_rule: If success threshold met → validate demand signal; if failure threshold met → weaken H-EDU-1 and reassess positioning; if inconclusive → extend test duration
experiments.risk_check: No public repositioning authorized. Landing page must be clearly marked as pilot preview. No payment collection.
experiments.instrumentation: Manual recording in experiments.json. Analytics via page view counter. No automated tracking.
experiments.claim_being_tested: Educators who see the education-focused landing page will sign up for a pilot at >=3% conversion rate

experiments.method: Recruit 5 game theory/strategy professors. 30-minute demo + structured interview. Ask: current tools, pain points, reaction to SIP, willingness to pilot, pricing comfort.
experiments.failure_threshold: 0 of 5 say they would pilot OR all say current tools are sufficient
experiments.decision_rule: If success threshold met → advance H-EDU-1 to pilot planning; if failure threshold met → disprove H-EDU-1 and recycle to Phase 3; if inconclusive → expand sample
experiments.risk_check: No outreach authorized without user approval. Interviews are voluntary. No product claims beyond demonstrated features.
experiments.instrumentation: Manual recording in experiments.json. Interview notes and recordings. No automated tracking.
experiments.claim_being_tested: >=3 of 5 professors say they would use SIP in their course after seeing a demo

experiments.method: Deploy SIP in 1-2 game theory courses for a semester. Professor assigns scenarios, students use platform, collect feedback surveys + professor interview at end.
experiments.failure_threshold: <50% student satisfaction OR professor would not continue
experiments.decision_rule: If success threshold met → support H-EDU-2 and advance to scale; if failure threshold met → weaken H-EDU-2 and recycle to Phase 4 for product gap remediation; if inconclusive → extend pilot
experiments.risk_check: Requires user approval and IRB review. No student PII stored beyond course context. Build must pass before deployment.
experiments.instrumentation: Manual recording in experiments.json. Student feedback surveys. Professor interview. Usage logs from Supabase.
experiments.claim_being_tested: Students in a pilot course using SIP will report >=70% satisfaction and the professor will report >=80% likelihood to continue using

experiments.method: Present at 1-2 academic conferences (Economic Science Association, Game Theory Society). Demo platform, distribute pilot sign-up sheet.
experiments.success_threshold: >=10 educators sign up for pilot info
experiments.failure_threshold: 0 signups OR attendees dismiss the approach
experiments.decision_rule: If success threshold met → validate distribution channel; if failure threshold met → reassess distribution strategy not product; if inconclusive → try different conference
experiments.risk_check: Requires user approval. Conference submission costs. No product claims beyond demonstrated features.
experiments.instrumentation: Manual recording in experiments.json. Sign-up sheet. Conference feedback.
experiments.claim_being_tested: >=10 educators express interest in piloting after conference presentation

experiments.method: 1) Request Feynn.ai demo. 2) Map feature-by-feature comparison. 3) Test game theory depth. 4) Assess evidence verification. 5) Document defensible differentiators.
experiments.failure_threshold: Feynn covers all SIP differentiators or Feynn's game theory is strictly deeper
experiments.decision_rule: If success threshold met → support H-GOV-2 differentiation claim; if failure threshold met → weaken H-GOV-2 and recycle to Phase 3 for new niche; if inconclusive → extend research
experiments.risk_check: No external commitments. Demo request only. No product claims beyond verified features.
experiments.instrumentation: Manual recording in experiments.json. Feature comparison spreadsheet. Demo notes.
experiments.claim_being_tested: SIP's forecast governance + human review workflow + education features are defensible differentiators that Feynn.ai cannot replicate without a major pivot

experiments.method: Five education-complement discovery calls: game-theory/economics professor, strategic-management or negotiation instructor, department/program owner, instructional/LMS lead, assessment or teaching-center lead. Manual, human-reviewed outreach only.
experiments.success_threshold: >=3/5 confirm a recurring job that current tools do not solve, name a buyer/champion, and request specific proof
experiments.failure_threshold: <2/5 confirm a recurring job OR >=3/5 say MobLab or incumbent tools are sufficient
experiments.decision_rule: If success threshold met AND >=2 qualified follow-ups → advance H-EDU-4 to validation; if failure threshold met → weaken H-EDU-4 and recycle; if inconclusive → redesign targeting
experiments.risk_check: No external outreach authorized without user approval. No PII collected beyond consent. No public messaging or pricing changes. Education positioning must be complementary to incumbent classroom tooling (MobLab), not replacement.
experiments.instrumentation: Manual recording per call: workflow, recurring pain, current substitute, frequency/cost, buyer/champion, switching barrier, proof requested, objection, WTP/procurement signal, next action, consent status, redacted evidence note. Stored in buyer artifact.
experiments.claim_being_tested: Education buyers need an evidence/governance complement to existing game-theory classroom tools (MobLab), not a replacement

experiments.method: Five enterprise/public-sector discovery calls: strategic-intelligence lead, geopolitical-risk lead, public-sector policy/foresight owner, executive briefing lead, procurement/governance owner. Manual, human-reviewed outreach only.
experiments.success_threshold: >=3/5 confirm a recurring strategic-analysis job, name a buyer/champion, and request specific proof
experiments.failure_threshold: <2/5 confirm a recurring job OR >=3/5 say current internal processes are sufficient
experiments.decision_rule: If success threshold met AND >=2 qualified follow-ups → advance H-ENT-0 to validation; if failure threshold met → weaken H-ENT-0 and recycle; if inconclusive → redesign targeting
experiments.risk_check: No external outreach authorized without user approval. No PII collected beyond consent. No public messaging or pricing changes. No procurement readiness claims from discovery calls alone.
experiments.instrumentation: Manual recording per call: workflow, recurring pain, current substitute, frequency/cost, buyer/champion, switching barrier, proof requested, objection, WTP/procurement signal, next action, consent status, redacted evidence note. Stored in buyer artifact.
experiments.claim_being_tested: Enterprise and public-sector teams need governed game-theoretic analysis with evidence provenance and audit trails
